title: Income Growth vs Discipline
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you maintain discipline while pursuing income growth?
What balance between spending and saving feels sustainable for your situation?

options.label: Income growth alone
options.reflection: While increasing income is important, focusing only on growth without discipline often leads to lifestyle inflation and financial stress.

options.label: Discipline + income growth
options.reflection: Exactly! Combining discipline with income growth creates a foundation for long-term financial success and security.

options.label: Spending everything you earn
options.reflection: Spending everything prevents building financial security and eliminates the benefits of income growth for your future self.

options.label: Saving everything without growth
options.reflection: While saving is important, focusing only on saving without pursuing income growth limits your financial potential unnecessarily.

stages.prompt: Why is discipline important when income increases?

options.label: It protects your money at all levels
options.reflection: Perfect! Discipline ensures that as your income grows, you're building security and making thoughtful decisions rather than just spending more.

options.label: It restricts your freedom to enjoy money
options.reflection: Actually, discipline creates more freedom by preventing financial stress and building options for your future choices.

options.label: It makes money management more complex
options.reflection: Discipline actually simplifies money management by creating consistent, reliable habits that work at any income level.

options.label: It's only important for lower incomes
options.reflection: Discipline is essential at all income levels - high earners without discipline often face the same financial challenges as low earners without discipline.

stages.prompt: What happens when you focus only on earning more?

options.label: Financial security automatically improves
options.reflection: Security doesn't happen automatically - it requires disciplined saving and spending decisions regardless of income level.

options.label: You feel more satisfied with your money
options.reflection: Without discipline, higher income often brings more stress about spending choices rather than satisfaction with financial progress.

options.label: Lifestyle inflation matches income growth
options.reflection: Exactly! Without discipline, people tend to increase spending proportionally to income, eliminating the benefits of growth.

options.label: Money management skills develop naturally
options.reflection: Money management skills must be deliberately developed - they don't automatically emerge with higher income.

stages.prompt: How should you handle a significant income increase?

options.label: Maintain the same savings and spending ratios
options.reflection: Excellent! Keeping consistent ratios ensures that discipline scales with your income, building security proportional to your earnings.

options.label: Increase spending to match your new status
options.reflection: Adjusting spending to 'match status' often leads to lifestyle inflation that eliminates the benefits of your increased income.

options.label: Save everything until you adjust to the new income
options.reflection: While caution is wise, overly restrictive saving can lead to resentment and make it harder to maintain long-term financial discipline.

options.label: Delay any changes for several months
options.reflection: Delaying decisions can lead to missed opportunities to build beneficial financial habits during the transition to higher income.

options.label: Focus only on increasing income
options.reflection: Income growth without discipline often leads to spending increases that eliminate the benefits of higher earnings.

options.label: Extreme caution with any spending
options.reflection: While discipline is important, extreme caution can prevent enjoying the benefits of your success and building confidence in financial decisions.

options.label: Decide that current income is enough
options.reflection: Stopping growth efforts limits your potential and may not provide sufficient resources for long-term financial goals.

options.label: Balanced discipline with growth pursuit
options.reflection: Perfect! This combination ensures you're building good habits while also expanding your earning potential for maximum long-term wealth.
